heading_path: 📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > ✨ Overview
text:
[📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > ✨ Overview]
YT-Bot is a powerful Next.js 14 application that revolutionizes how you interact with YouTube video content. It allows users to submit YouTube video URLs and then leverage the cutting-edge Gemini 2.0 Flash API to ask intelligent questions about the video's content. Utilizing Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), the app extracts transcripts, generates embeddings, and performs vector similarity searches to provide accurate and context-aware answers.

heading_path: 📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > 🚀 Features
text:
[📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > 🚀 Features]
*   **Intelligent Q&A:** Ask natural language questions about any YouTube video and get precise answers powered by Gemini 2.0 Flash.
*   **Comment Sentiment Analysis:** Analyze YouTube video comments with VADER sentiment analysis, spam detection, and filtering.
*   **Robust Transcript Generation:** Automatically fetches YouTube video transcripts. If no transcript is available, it uses AssemblyAI to convert the video's audio to text.
*   **Semantic Search:** Chunks and embeds transcripts, enabling vector similarity search for highly relevant content retrieval.
*   **User Authentication:** Secure user management powered by JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for seamless sign-up and sign-in.
*   **Video Management:** Easily add, view details, and delete your submitted YouTube videos.
*   **Question History:** Keep track of all your questions and their corresponding AI-generated answers for each video.
*   **Rate Limiting:** Implements rate limiting to ensure efficient API usage and stay within free tier limits.

heading_path: 📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > 🛠️ Tech Stack
text:
[📺 YT-Bot: YouTube Video Q&A App > 🛠️ Tech Stack]
*   **Frontend:** Next.js 14 (App Router), React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
*   **Backend:** Next.js API Routes + FastAPI (Python) for comment analysis
*   **Database:** NeonDB (PostgreSQL with `pg_vector` extension)
*   **ORM:** Prisma
*   **Authentication:** JWT (JSON Web Tokens)
*   **AI/LLM:** Google Gemini 2.0 Flash API
*   **Embeddings:** Google Gemini `text-embedding-001`
*   **Sentiment Analysis:** VADER (Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner)
*   **YouTube APIs:** YouTube Data API v3 (for comments), YouTube Transcript API
*   **Speech-to-Text:** AssemblyAI
*   **Video Processing:** `@langchain/community/document_loaders/web/youtube` for video metadata and transcript loading.

heading_path: Comment Analyzer API URL (for local development) > Installation
text:
[Comment Analyzer API URL (for local development) > Installation]
1.  **Clone the repository:**
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yourusername/yt-bot.git
    cd yt-bot
    ```
    *(Replace `yourusername/yt-bot.git` with your actual repository URL if you've forked it)*

2.  **Install dependencies:**
    ```bash
    npm install
    ```

3.  **Set up the database:**
    Ensure your `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` is correct. Then, apply Prisma migrations to set up your database schema:
    ```bash
    npx prisma migrate dev --name create_initial_tables
    ```
    *(If you already have migrations, use `npx prisma migrate deploy`)*

4.  **Run the development server:**

**Option A: Run everything at once (Recommended for Windows)**
    ```bash
    .\dev.bat
    ```
    This will start both the Comment Analyzer API (port 7860) and the Next.js frontend (port 3000) in separate terminal windows.

**Option B: Manual start**
    ```bash
    npm run dev
    ```
    (Note: If using the Comment Analyzer feature, you'll need to start it separately - see below)

5.  **Access the application:**
    Open your web browser and navigate to [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

1.  **Sign Up / Sign In:** Register for a new account or log in using your JWT-based authentication.
2.  **Add a YouTube Video:** On the dashboard, paste a YouTube video URL into the input field and submit it.
3.  **Processing:** The application will process the video, extracting its transcript (or generating one via AssemblyAI) and generating embeddings. This might take a moment depending on the video length.

[Repo: automate-on-edge]
**Purpose:** An agentic AI verification system that turns datasheets into test plans, controls Arduino-connected hardware over serial, and automatically analyzes the resulting measurements.

**Primary language:** Python

**Repository:** https://github.com/saadrizvi09/automate-on-edge

**Created:** 2026-02-20 · **Last pushed:** 2026-05-09

[AI Product Verification Engineer Agent (AI-PVE)]
> **One PDF in, one validated DVP report out — across arbitrary 74-series logic ICs on real or simulated hardware.**

An autonomous verification engineer for commodity logic ICs. Drop a datasheet PDF, pick the device profile (or let the extractor auto-detect it), confirm the bench wiring, and the agent will derive testable requirements, generate an Arduino sketch, flash the bench, stream readings, run SPC + follow-up reruns, and emit a multi-page DVP PDF that cites measured voltages and proposes root causes for any anomaly.

heading_path: AI Product Verification Engineer Agent (AI-PVE) > What it does
text:
[AI Product Verification Engineer Agent (AI-PVE) > What it does]
1. **Datasheet → requirements** — PyMuPDF + LLM extracts measurable thresholds (V_OH, V_OL, V_IH, V_IL, propagation delay, supply range) and detects intra-spec conflicts.
2. **Profile auto-detect** — A regex part-number detector reads the PDF text and pre-fills the active `DeviceProfile` from the registry (74LS00/02/04/08/32/86, 74HC32 ship out of the box). Manual override via dropdown is always available.
3. **Pre-flash wiring checklist** — Per-gate checkboxes drive the run scope. Tick only the gates you actually wired; floating analog inputs on un-jumpered gates are filtered out before they ever reach the agent.
4. **Test plan + sketch** — LLM emits a verification plan; sketch generator produces a profile-driven Arduino sketch with the SKETCH-04/05 bug fixes (pinMode coverage, no `analogReference(EXTERNAL)`). LLM output is structurally validated; if the LLM produces invalid C++ or invents its own pin scheme, the deterministic profile-driven generator takes over.
5. **Live execution** — `arduino-cli` flashes the Uno; readings stream over serial as NDJSON to the dashboard. Hardware disconnects mid-stream surface as an `error` SSE frame ("Arduino disconnected"), not a hung pipeline.
6. **Analysis + adaptive follow-up** — SPC computes Cpk per (gate, A, B) condition using the active profile's V_OH_min/V_OL_max. The follow-up agent schedules focused reruns on weak conditions; reruns scoped to unwired gates are skipped automatically.
7. **DVP PDF report** — Cover narrative, Device Under Test section (pin map + thresholds + datasheet citation), Requirements, Conflicts, Test Results, Failure Analysis, SPC, Follow-up, **Measured Evidence (per-condition voltages with margins)**, **Diagnostics & Potential Issues (per-anomaly root-cause hypothesis + recommended action)**, and Sign-off.
8. **Natural-language Q&A** — Chat panel answers questions about the run, citing the active profile + datasheet ref. Profile context flows end-to-end so 74LS32 runs are never confused with 7400 NAND.

heading_path: AI Product Verification Engineer Agent (AI-PVE) > Device profile registry
text:
[AI Product Verification Engineer Agent (AI-PVE) > Device profile registry]
`backend/devices/profiles/*.json` ships with seven profiles:

| Profile | Family | Function | Gates | Inputs/gate | V_OH_min | V_OL_max | Datasheet |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 74LS00 | LS | NAND | 4 | 2 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS027C |
| 74LS02 | LS | NOR | 4 | 2 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS028C |
| 74LS04 | LS | NOT | 6 | 1 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS029C |
| 74LS08 | LS | AND | 4 | 2 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS030C |
| 74LS32 | LS | OR | 4 | 2 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS079C *(bench-validated reference)* |
| 74LS86 | LS | XOR | 4 | 2 | 2.7 V | 0.4 V | TI SDLS081C |
| 74HC32 | HC | OR | 4 | 2 | **4.4 V** | **0.1 V** | TI SDLS090C *(cross-family demonstrator)* |

Each profile includes the IC pin map and the bench `arduino_pin_map`. The 74LS32 entry encodes the validated wiring `pin1=D2, pin2=D3, pin3=A0, pin7=GND, pin14=5V` extended consistently to all four gates. Adding a new chip is a single JSON file; the schema is enforced via Pydantic v2 cross-field validators (V_OL_max < V_OH_min, pin-count vs package, no duplicate pin numbers, etc.). Aliases work (`get_profile("7400")` → 74LS00; `SN74LS32N` → 74LS32) so vendor prefixes / package suffixes don't trip lookups.

[Terminal 2 > Bench wiring (74LS32 reference)]
Notch left, IC pins → Arduino:

| IC pin | Function | Arduino |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Gate 1 input A | D2 |
| 2 | Gate 1 input B | D3 |
| 3 | Gate 1 output | A0 |
| 4 | Gate 2 input A | D4 |
| 5 | Gate 2 input B | D5 |
| 6 | Gate 2 output | A1 |
| 9 | Gate 3 input A | D6 |
| 10 | Gate 3 input B | D7 |
| 8 | Gate 3 output | A2 |
| 12 | Gate 4 input A | D8 |
| 13 | Gate 4 input B | D9 |
| 11 | Gate 4 output | A3 |
| 7 | GND | GND |
| 14 | VCC | 5V |

You don't have to wire all four gates. The pre-flash checklist exposes one row per gate; tick only what you've jumpered, and the executor + analyser only see those gates. Floating analog inputs settle around mid-rail (~2 V) and would otherwise produce phantom anomalies — this avoids that entirely.

[Terminal 2 > API surface (highlights)]
- `POST /api/extract-requirements` — PDF in (raw bytes), requirements + auto-detected profile out. `x-device-profile-id` header overrides auto-detect.
- `POST /api/generate-test-plan` — requirements → test cases.
- `POST /api/generate-script` — test plan → profile-driven Arduino sketch.
- `POST /api/flash-arduino` — uploads sketch via arduino-cli.
- `POST /api/stream-execution` — NDJSON SSE; emits `reading`, `anomaly`, `complete`, `error` events. Accepts `gates_under_test: int[]`.
- `POST /api/analyse-results` — readings → SPC + failure list + adaptive follow-up plan.
- `POST /api/run-follow-up` — executes targeted reruns. Skips plans for unwired gates.
- `POST /api/generate-report` — full DVP PDF (cover, requirements, results, SPC, **measured evidence**, **diagnostics**, sign-off).
- `POST /api/ask-results` — natural-language Q&A over the run, profile-aware citations.
- `GET  /api/profiles` — list all 7 profiles, sorted by part_number.
- `GET  /api/profiles/{id}` — single profile (alias-aware).
- `GET  /api/hardware-status` — port visibility + openable check.
- `GET  /api/model-status` — active LLM provider + fallback state.
- `GET  /api/runs/{id}` — run record + event log (when persistence enabled).

All routes accept an optional `profile_id`; legacy callers without it preserve the byte-identical pre-Milestone-1 7400 NAND behaviour.

[Terminal 2 > Operational notes]
- **`arduino-cli` PATH gotcha** — running processes (uvicorn, npm) keep the `PATH` they were started with. After installing arduino-cli, restart uvicorn from a fresh shell, or pin `ARDUINO_CLI_PATH` in `.env` to the absolute exe path.
- **Sketch directory convention** — arduino-cli requires `<dir>/<dir>.ino`. Sketches now write to `arduino/generated/generated.ino` to satisfy this.
- **Serial timeout scaling** — the timeout for serial reads is `max(30s, expected_count × 6s)`. Focused follow-up reads (30 samples on one condition) get 180 s, plenty of headroom even with a slow inter-sweep delay on the Arduino.
- **Sketch compile guards** — LLM-generated sketches that emit `[][]` (invalid multi-dim array) or that don't reference the prompt's `GATE0_INPUT_A` constants are rejected; the deterministic profile-driven generator runs instead. The deterministic path is provably correct for every shipped profile and embeds the SKETCH-04/05 bug fixes.
- **Markdown fences in LLM JSON** — `coerce_json_text()` in `agents/_prompt_helpers.py` strips ` ``` ` and ` ```json ` fences from LLM JSON output before `json.loads`. Applies to extractor, analyser, report writer, Q&A.

[Terminal 2 > Product direction]
The strongest commercial direction is **AI-driven incoming inspection and adaptive screening for commodity and legacy ICs** — automatically screening purchased ICs, detecting counterfeit / drifting batches, and removing manual datasheet-to-test work from the inspection floor.

[WagerKit 🎯]
**Real-time prediction market analysis with integrity scoring**

WagerKit aggregates data from multiple prediction market sources (Polymarket, Kalshi, PredictIt) and provides comprehensive integrity analysis for each market.

[WagerKit 🎯 > ✨ Features]
- 📈 **Multi-Source Aggregation**: Compare odds across leading prediction markets
- 🎯 **Integrity Scoring**: 4-component analysis (Clarity, Liquidity, Agreement, Volatility)
- 📊 **Visual Analytics**: Real-time odds history charts with Chart.js
- 📥 **Export Tools**: PDF dossiers, CSV data exports, JSON metadata
- ⚡ **Background Processing**: BullMQ-powered workers for instant dashboard loads
- 🎨 **Modern UI**: Dark theme, responsive design with Tailwind CSS

[WagerKit 🎯 > 🏗️ Architecture]
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│      Docker Container (HF Space)    │
│                                     │
│  Redis ──► NestJS Backend (BullMQ) │
│              ↓                      │
│         Next.js Frontend            │
│           (Port 7860)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Git-Bot is a Next.js application designed to leverage AI capabilities for enhanced GitHub interactions. It integrates with various AI models and GitHub's API to provide intelligent automation and assistance for developers.

[Git-Bot > Features]
*   **AI-Powered GitHub Interactions:** Utilize advanced AI models for tasks such as code review suggestions, issue summarization, or intelligent pull request descriptions.
*   **Next.js Framework:** Built with Next.js for a performant and scalable web application.
*   **Clerk for Authentication:** Secure user authentication and management powered by Clerk.
*   **Prisma ORM:** Seamless database integration and management with Prisma.
*   **tRPC for API:** Type-safe end-to-end communication with tRPC.
*   **Radix UI & Tailwind CSS:** Modern and accessible UI components styled with Tailwind CSS.
*   **Octokit Integration:** Direct interaction with the GitHub API using Octokit.
*   **Google Gemini & Mistral AI Integration:** Utilizes AI models from Google Gemini and Mistral AI for various intelligent features.

[Git-Bot > Technologies Used]
*   [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/)
*   [React](https://react.dev/)
*   [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
*   [Clerk](https://clerk.com/)
*   [Prisma](https://www.prisma.io/)
*   [tRPC](https://trpc.io/)
*   [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/)
*   [Radix UI](https://www.radix-ui.com/)
*   [Octokit](https://octokit.github.io/)
*   [@ai-sdk/google](https://ai.google.dev/): Google AI SDK
*   [@google/generative-ai](https://ai.google.dev/): Google Generative AI
*   [@mistralai/mistralai](https://mistral.ai/): Mistral AI
*   [Zod](https://zod.dev/): Schema validation
*   [Tanstack Query](https://tanstack.com/query/latest): Data fetching and caching

[Git-Bot > Getting Started]
Follow these steps to set up and run the project locally.

[Git-Bot > Getting Started > Prerequisites]
*   Node.js (v18 or higher)
*   Bun (or npm/yarn)
*   Git
*   A GitHub account and a Personal Access Token (PAT) with appropriate permissions.
*   API keys for Google Gemini and/or Mistral AI (if utilizing AI features).

[Git-Bot > Getting Started > Installation]
1.  **Clone the repository:**

```bash
    git clone https://github.com/your-username/git-bot.git
    cd git-bot
    ```

2.  **Install dependencies:**

```bash
    bun install
    # or npm install
    # or yarn install
    ```

3.  **Set up environment variables:**

Create a `.env` file in the root directory based on `.env.example`.

```
    # .env.example
    # Clerk
    NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY="pk_test_..."
    CLERK_SECRET_KEY="sk_test_..."

# GitHub
    GITHUB_CLIENT_ID="your_github_client_id"
    GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET="your_github_client_secret"

# Prisma
    DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@host:port/database"

# Google Gemini
    GOOGLE_API_KEY="your_google_api_key"

# Mistral AI
    MISTRAL_API_KEY="your_mistral_api_key"
    ```

Replace the placeholder values with your actual keys and credentials.

4.  **Database Setup:**

Run Prisma migrations to set up your database schema:

```bash
    bun run db:migrate
    # or npm run db:migrate
    ```

If you want to push the schema without migrations (e.g., for development), use:

```bash
    bun run db:push
    # or npm run db:push
    ```

You can also open Prisma Studio to view your database:

```bash
    bun run db:studio
    # or npm run db:studio
    ```

[Enable debug logging > Log Files]
Log files are automatically created in the `logs/` directory with timestamps:

```

```

Each log file contains:
- Timestamps
- Log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
- Function names and line numbers
- API requests and responses (sensitive data masked)
- Error details and stack traces

[Enable debug logging > Error Handling]
The bot handles various error scenarios:

- **Invalid input**: Validation errors with clear messages
- **API errors**: Binance API error codes and messages
- **Network errors**: Timeout and connection issues
- **Authentication errors**: Invalid or missing credentials

Example error output:

```

==================================================

[Enable debug logging > Testing]
The bot has been tested and verified working with:
- ✅ Price queries (BTCUSDT: $78,166.00 retrieved successfully)
- ✅ API authentication with Binance Futures Testnet
- ✅ Input validation and error handling
- ✅ Logging to file with timestamps
- ✅ All sample log files included in `logs/` directory

[Enable debug logging > Assumptions]
1. **Testnet only**: This bot is designed for Binance Futures Testnet only. Do not use with production API keys.

2. **USDT-M Futures**: Designed for USDT-margined futures pairs (e.g., BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT).

3. **Minimum quantities**: Binance has minimum notional value requirements. For BTCUSDT, typical minimum is 0.001 BTC.

4. **No position management**: The bot places orders but does not implement automated trading strategies.

5. **Single orders**: The bot places one order at a time (no batch orders).

heading_path: Enable debug logging > Troubleshooting > "API credentials not found"
text:
[Enable debug logging > Troubleshooting > "API credentials not found"]
Make sure you have:
1. Created a `.env` file in the project directory
2. Added both `BINANCE_API_KEY` and `BINANCE_API_SECRET`
3. No extra spaces around the `=` sign

[Enable debug logging > Troubleshooting > "Invalid symbol"]
Check that:
1. Symbol exists on Binance Futures Testnet
2. Symbol is uppercase (e.g., `BTCUSDT` not `btcusdt`)
3. Symbol includes the quote currency (e.g., `BTCUSDT` not `BTC`)

[Enable debug logging > Troubleshooting > "Insufficient margin"]
The testnet account may need funds:
1. Go to [Binance Futures Testnet](https://testnet.binancefuture.com/)
2. Request test funds from the faucet (if available)
3. Or reduce order quantity

There are several ways of editing your application.

**Use Lovable**

Simply visit the [Lovable Project](https://lovable.dev/projects/ef12a869-7b8d-4d2f-80cd-5d18589d63f1) and start prompting.

Changes made via Lovable will be committed automatically to this repo.

**Use your preferred IDE**

If you want to work locally using your own IDE, you can clone this repo and push changes. Pushed changes will also be reflected in Lovable.

The only requirement is having Node.js & npm installed - [install with nvm](https://github.com/nvm-sh/nvm#installing-and-updating)

text:
[🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > 🎯 Overview]
AlgoQuant is a **full-stack quantitative trading platform** featuring HMM-SVR (Hidden Markov Model + Support Vector Regression) for long-term cryptocurrency trading. The bot checks every **3 hours** for optimal entry/exit points using AI-driven regime detection and volatility prediction.

**Why AlgoQuant?**
- 🤖 **AI-Driven** - HMM regime detection + SVR volatility prediction
- 📊 **Long-Term Focus** - 3-hour intervals, position holding for days/weeks
- 🎯 **Auto-Training** - Models train automatically on first run (no manual setup)
- 💼 **Risk-Managed** - Dynamic position sizing (0x/1x/3x) based on regime confidence
- 🆓 **Zero Cost** - Free APIs (Yahoo Finance), no subscriptions
- 🔒 **Secure** - JWT auth, bcrypt hashing, isolated portfolios
- ⚡ **Deployed** - Live on Hugging Face Spaces

heading_path: 🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > � HMM-SVR Strategy Explained > 🧠 What is HMM-SVR?
text:
[🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > � HMM-SVR Strategy Explained > 🧠 What is HMM-SVR?]
**HMM (Hidden Markov Model)** detects market regimes:
- **State 0 (Safe)**: Low volatility, stable trends → High confidence trades
- **State 1 (Neutral)**: Normal market conditions → Standard trading
- **State 2 (Crash)**: High volatility, panic selling → Exit all positions

**SVR (Support Vector Regression)** predicts future volatility to assess risk.

heading_path: 🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > � HMM-SVR Strategy Explained > 🔄 How It Works (Every 3 Hours)
text:
[🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > � HMM-SVR Strategy Explained > 🔄 How It Works (Every 3 Hours)]
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Fetch Price Data] --> B[Calculate EMAs 12/26]
    B --> C[Calculate RSI]
    C --> D[Calculate Volatility]
    D --> E[HMM Regime Detection]
    E --> F[SVR Volatility Prediction]
    F --> G{Regime + Trend?}
    G -->|Safe + Bullish + Low Vol| H[BUY 3x]
    G -->|Neutral + Bullish| I[BUY 1x]
    G -->|Crash or Bearish| J[SELL/HOLD]
```

heading_path: 🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > 🎲 Trading Strategies(will add more soon) > 1. HMM-SVR Leverage Strategy (Walk-Forward) ⭐ Flagship
text:
[🚀 AlgoQuant - AI-Powered Crypto Trading Platform > 🎲 Trading Strategies(will add more soon) > 1. HMM-SVR Leverage Strategy (Walk-Forward) ⭐ Flagship]
**The most sophisticated strategy with zero lookahead bias through strict walk-forward simulation.**

**Architecture:**
```
Day-by-Day Simulation → 252-Day Sliding Window → HMM Regime + SVR Volatility → Dynamic Leverage
```

**How It Works:**
1. **HMM Regime Detection** - Classifies market into 3 states:
   - State 0: Low Volatility (Safe) ✅
   - State 1: Normal Volatility (Neutral) ⚠️
   - State 2: High Volatility (Crash) ❌

2. **SVR Volatility Prediction** - Forecasts next-day volatility risk using current features

3. **Dynamic Leverage System:**
   - **0x Leverage** - Exit all positions in crash regimes (State 2)
   - **1x Leverage** - Normal trading in neutral markets (State 1)
   - **3x Leverage** - Amplify gains when certain (State 0 + Risk_Ratio < 0.5)

4. **EMA Crossover Signals** - 12/26 EMA for trend confirmation

**Why "Walk-Forward"?**
- Each prediction uses ONLY data available up to that moment
- No future data leakage or hindsight bias
- Simulates real trading conditions day-by-day
- Results are achievable in live markets

**Configuration:**
- `short_window`: 12 (Fast EMA)
- `long_window`: 26 (Slow EMA)
- `n_states`: 3 (HMM regime states)
- `lookback_window`: 252 days (1 trading year)

**Metrics:** Total Return, Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar Ratios, Max Drawdown, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Average Leverage

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech]
**Purpose:** Voice chat with AI about any image(click pic using camera) on Raspberry PI

**Primary language:** Python

**Repository:** https://github.com/saadrizvi09/ros-image-to-speech

**Created:** 2025-10-21 · **Last pushed:** 2025-10-21

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > README]

V.O.I.D. - Visual Object Identification and Description

--- This project is a ROS 2 application that allows a user to have a real-time, voice-controlled conversation about the contents of an image. It uses Google's Gemini AI for visual understanding and generating responses, which are then spoken back to the user via text-to-speech.

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > 🚀 Key Features] * 🗣️ **Voice-Controlled Queries:** Ask questions about an image using your voice. * 🧠 **Real-time AI Responses:** Leverages the Gemini AI model for advanced image understanding. * 🔊 **Text-to-Speech Output:** The AI's answers are spoken back to you in natural-sounding language. * 🛑 **Interruptible Speech:** Say "stop" at any time to immediately interrupt the AI's response. * 🚪 **Graceful Shutdown:** Say "end" to shut down the entire application. * 🚀 **Centralized Launch:** The entire system is managed and started with a single ROS 2 launch file.

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > 🛠️ System Architecture] The application runs as a distributed system with two main nodes communicating over ROS 2 topics. This modular design allows for easy extension and maintenance. * **`speech_node` (Speech Input):** * Listens to the microphone for user speech. * Converts speech-to-text using Google's Web Speech API. * Publishes user questions to the `/user_query` topic. * Publishes control commands ("stop", "end") to the `/speech_command` topic. * **`vision_node` (AI & Speech Output):** * Subscribes to `/user_query` to receive questions. * Subscribes to `/speech_command` to receive control commands. * Sends the image and the user's question to the Gemini AI API. * Receives the text response from the AI. * Uses text-to-speech to speak the response aloud.
---

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > ⚙️ Installation Guide]
Follow these steps to set up and run the project on your local machine.

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > ⚙️ Installation Guide > 1. Prerequisites]
* Ubuntu 24.04 with ROS 2 Jazzy Jalisco installed.
* Python 3.12+.
* A working microphone configured on your system.

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > ⚙️ Installation Guide > 2. Setup] 1. **Clone the Repository** ```bash # Navigate to your ROS 2 workspace's src directory cd ~/ros2_ws/src git clone ``` 2. **Install Python Dependencies** ```bash pip install google-generativeai gtts sounddevice soundfile SpeechRecognition PyAudio Pillow python-dotenv --break-system-packages sudo apt update && sudo apt install ffmpeg ``` 3. **Configure API Key** * Create a `.env` file in the root of your ROS 2 workspace (`~/ros2_ws`). * Add your Google AI API key to this file: ``` GOOGLE_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY_HERE" ``` 4. **Build the Workspace** ```bash cd ~/ros2_ws colcon build --packages-select gemini_ros ```
---

[Repo: ros-image-to-speech > ▶️ How to Run]
The entire application can be started from a single terminal using the provided launch file.

1.  **Source the Workspace**
    Open a terminal and navigate to your workspace root:
    ```bash
    cd ~/ros2_ws
    source install/setup.bash
    ```

2.  **Run the Launch File**
    **Note:** Before running, you must edit the `src/gemini_ros/launch/gemini_app.launch.py` file to set the correct path to your image.
    ```bash
    ros2 launch gemini_ros gemini_app.launch.py
    ```
The system will start, calibrate the microphone for ambient noise, and begin listening for your voice commands. To stop the application, press `Ctrl+C` in the terminal or say "end".

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 📊 Overview
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 📊 Overview]
The **Hybrid HMM-SVR Regime Detector** is a specialized financial analytics tool designed to forecast cryptocurrency volatility by understanding market context.

Unlike traditional linear models, this application implements a **Hybrid Machine Learning Architecture** that combines Unsupervised Learning (**Hidden Markov Models**) with Supervised Learning (**Support Vector Regression**).

> **Scientific Consensus:** This architecture is strictly aligned with findings from a **Systematic Literature Review (2022)** on cryptocurrency volatility. The review identified Markov-Switching models as the consensus method for regime detection and SVM/SVR as a top-performing technique for non-linear forecasting.

---

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis]
The methodology is derived from three core concepts in quantitative finance literature:

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 1. Regime Detection (HMM)
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 1. Regime Detection (HMM)]
Financial markets are not stationary; they switch between latent states (e.g., "Steady Bull" vs. "Panic Crash"). Standard models fail to capture these structural breaks. We use **Gaussian Hidden Markov Models (HMM)** to probabilistically identify these hidden states based on observed returns and volatility. Below is the regime detection for a volatile coin like Solana

![HMM Regime Detection](image-2.png)
*Figure 1: HMM delineating market regimes (Bullish vs. Bearish/Crash).*

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 2. Volatility Forecasting (SVR)
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 2. Volatility Forecasting (SVR)]
While GARCH is the traditional econometric approach, modern literature (*Peng et al. 2018, Aras 2021*) suggests that **Support Vector Regression (SVR)** frequently outperforms GARCH in crypto markets by capturing complex, non-linear patterns inherent in digital assets.

![SVR Forecasting](image-1.png)
*Figure 2: SVR capturing non-linear volatility trends compared to actuals.*

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 3. The Leverage Effect
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🔬 Scientific Basis > 3. The Leverage Effect]
Markets exhibit "Asymmetric Volatility"—bad news (price drops) tends to generate significantly higher future volatility than good news. This model engineers specific features to capture this **"Downside Impact"** or "Smirk. (Notice the red points)"

![Leverage Effect](image.png)
*Figure 3: The Leverage Effect (Volatility Smile/Smirk) analysis.*

---

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🚀 Key Features
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🚀 Key Features]
| Feature | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Unsupervised Regime ID** | Automatically detects 2-4 hidden market states (Calm, Transition, Crash) and visualizes them directly on price charts. |
| **Hybrid Forecasting** | Uses the detected **Regime** as a context feature, feeding it into an SVR model to predict next-day volatility risk. |
| **Metric Tracking** | Real-time calculation of **MSE** (Mean Squared Error) and **MAE** (Mean Absolute Error) to validate model accuracy. |
| **Leverage Analysis** | Visualizes the "Volatility Smile" to empirically prove that negative returns drive higher risk in your specific dataset. |

---

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline]
The model follows a strict three-step pipeline to transform raw data into actionable risk insights.

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 1: Feature Engineering
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 1: Feature Engineering]
* Calculates **Log Returns** and **Rolling Volatility**.
* Computes a **Downside Volatility** feature to mathematically represent the leverage effect.

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 2: The "Historian" (Unsupervised HMM)
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 2: The "Historian" (Unsupervised HMM)]
* The HMM scans historical data and clusters days into "Regimes" based on statistical behavior.
* *Logic:* It sorts states such that **State 0 = Lowest Risk** and **State N = Highest Risk**.

heading_path: Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 3: The "Forecaster" (Supervised SVR)
text:
[Hybrid HMM-SVR Crypto Regime Detector > 🧠 The Hybrid Pipeline > Step 3: The "Forecaster" (Supervised SVR)]
* The SVR takes the **Regime Label** (from Step 2) combined with recent price action.
* It fits a regression tube (Epsilon-Tube) to predict tomorrow's volatility, minimizing error while ignoring insignificant market noise.

---

[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide]

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 📊 The Core Philosophy
text:
[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 📊 The Core Philosophy]
The entire strategy is built on one concept:
> **"Aggressive Discipline"**

DeepSeek combined two seemingly opposite traits:
* **Aggressive:** Take large, leveraged positions when an opportunity is clear.
* **Disciplined:** Follow strict risk rules and exit losing trades immediately.

---

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑
text:
[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑]
> **Rule:** Only trade in the direction of clear momentum.

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > How to Identify Momentum
text:
[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > How to Identify Momentum]
* Price making **higher highs and higher lows** (uptrend).
* **Volume increasing** during up-moves.
* Price **breaking above key resistance** levels.
* **RSI > 50** and rising.

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > Example
text:
[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > Example]
* BTC breaks $45,000 resistance with high volume.
* → **This is a BUY signal**, not a place to short.
* → Enter **long with 5-10x leverage**.
* → Target: $47,000 (4.4% gain = 22-44% profit with leverage).

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > ❌ What to Avoid
text:
[🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 1. Momentum Is King 👑 > ❌ What to Avoid]
* Trying to "catch falling knives."
* Shorting strong uptrends.
* Buying during clear downtrends.

text:

heading_path: 🏆 DeepSeek's Winning Trading Strategy - Complete Guide > 🎯 The 7 Commandments > 2. Size Matters 💪 > DeepSeek's Position Sizing
text:

[🚀 Getting started with Strapi]
Strapi comes with a full featured [Command Line Interface](https://docs.strapi.io/dev-docs/cli) (CLI) which lets you scaffold and manage your project in seconds.

[🚀 Getting started with Strapi > `develop`]
Start your Strapi application with autoReload enabled. [Learn more](https://docs.strapi.io/dev-docs/cli#strapi-develop)

```
npm run develop

[or]
yarn develop
```

[or > `start`]
Start your Strapi application with autoReload disabled. [Learn more](https://docs.strapi.io/dev-docs/cli#strapi-start)

```
npm run start

[or]
yarn start
```

[or > `build`]
Build your admin panel. [Learn more](https://docs.strapi.io/dev-docs/cli#strapi-build)

```
npm run build

[or]
yarn build
```

[or > ⚙️ Deployment]
Strapi gives you many possible deployment options for your project including [Strapi Cloud](https://cloud.strapi.io). Browse the [deployment section of the documentation](https://docs.strapi.io/dev-docs/deployment) to find the best solution for your use case.

```
yarn strapi deploy
```
